item.projectred_core.screwdriver.tooltip: Use on Project Red redstone components to rotate them.
item.projectred_integration.or_gate.tool:
Has 3 inputs and 1 output.
Output is on if at least 1 input is on.
item.projectred_integration.nor_gate.too:
Has 3 inputs and 1 output.
Output is on if all inputs are off.
item.projectred_integration.not_gate.too:
Has 1 input and 3 outputs.
Output is on if the input is off.
item.projectred_integration.and_gate.too:
Has 3 inputs and 1 output.
Output is on if all inputs are on.
item.projectred_integration.nand_gate.to:
Has 3 inputs and 1 output.
Output is on if at least 1 input is off.
item.projectred_integration.xor_gate.too:
Has 2 inputs and 1 output.
Output is on if only one of the inputs is on.
item.projectred_integration.xnor_gate.to:
Has 2 inputs and 1 output.
Output is on if both or none of the inputs are on.
Has 1 input and 3 outputs
Delays the signal by 1 tick.
Acts similar to a diode, where the signal can only pass in one direction.
item.projectred_integration.multiplexer_:
Has 3 inputs and 1 output
Acts like an XOR gate, but the back input acts as a selector for the other 2 inputs.
When the back input is off, the right input is selected, and when the back input is on, the left input is selected.
item.projectred_integration.pulse_gate.t:
Has 1 input and 1 output
When the input turns on, a 1 tick pulse is emitted.
Similar to observers, but does not emit a pulse when the input turns off.
item.projectred_integration.repeater_gat:
Has 1 input and 1 output
Delays the signal by a configurable amount of ticks.
Min: 1 tick Max: 256 ticks.
item.projectred_integration.randomizer_g:
Has 1 input and 3 outputs
When receiving a signal, randomly outputs any combination of the 3 outputs.
If the input is always on, it will continue randomizing the outputs every tick. Outputs remain active after pulse ends.
item.projectred_integration.sr_latch_gat:
Has 2 inputs and 2 outputs
Right input is for front output, and left input is for back output.
When the right input receives a signal, the front output turns on and remains on until the left input receives a signal, which turns off the front output and turns on the back output. The back output remains on until the right input receives a signal again. If both inputs receive a signal at the same time, the outputs will be both off.
item.projectred_integration.toggle_latch:
Has 2 inputs and 2 outputs, inputs are shared
When either input receives a signal, the outputs will swap states. If both inputs receive a signal at the same time, the outputs will not change.
item.projectred_integration.transparent_:
Has 2 inputs and 2 outputs
Back input is a write signal, and left input is a keep signal. Is similar to a 1-bit storage cell.
When the back input receives a signal, the left input state is stored and outputted. When the keep signal receives a signal, the current state will be kept until the next write signal. Chaining multiple transparent latch gates together can create a transmission chain of data, where the left latch will determine the state of the latch to the right of it on the next write signal.
item.projectred_integration.light_sensor:
Has 1 output
Outputs signal based on the light level
Shift-click with a screw driver to change the mode:
Open (both colors): All light sources
Half-open (blue): Only natural light sources
Closed (red): Only artificial light sources
item.projectred_integration.rain_sensor_:
Has 1 output
Outputs a signal when it is raining
Requires a clear view of the sky.
item.projectred_integration.timer_gate.t:
Has 1 inputs, 1 output and 2 shared IO sides
Click on it to configure the timer
It will output a pulse every time the timer reaches the configured time. If any of the inputs are active, the timer will reset and stop until all inputs are inactive again.
item.projectred_integration.sequencer_ga:
Has 4 outputs
Click on it to configure the timer
It will output from the sides in a clockwise order, with the configured time being the time a side is active before moving on to the next side.
item.projectred_integration.counter_gate:
Has 2 outputs, 1 positive and 1 negative input sides
Click on it to configure the counter
The positive and negativ inputs will add values to the counter, moving the indicator towards one of the outputs. When the indicator reaches an output, it will stop moving in that direction and turn on the output.
item.projectred_integration.state_cell_g:
Has 2 input and 2 outputs
Click on it to configure the timer
When the back input receives a pulse, the left output will stay on for the configured amount of time before turning off. When the output turns off, the front output will ouput a pulse. The right input will reset the timer back to 0.
item.projectred_integration.synchronizer:
Has 3 inputs and 1 output
It "remembers" if it received a signal from the left or the right. Once it has received a signal from both, it outputs a pulse and resets itself. the back input is a reset signal, which will reset the synchronizer without outputting a pulse.
item.projectred_integration.bus_transcie:
Has 2 inputs and 2 bundled shared IO sides
Blocks 2-way data transmission. Use inputs to enable a direction of transmission. It adds a delay of 1 tick. If both sides are enabled, it will run in a circular power race condition.
item.projectred_integration.null_cell_ga:
Has 2 pairs of shared IO sides
Acts as a separator of two intersecting lines.
item.projectred_integration.inverter_cel:
Has 2 pairs of shared IO sides
Powering on the lower pair will invert the upper pair, which is on by default.
item.projectred_integration.buffer_cell_:
Has 2 pairs of shared IO sides
Powering the lower side will also power the upper side.
item.projectred_integration.comparator_g:
Has 1 input, 1 output, 1 positive and 1 negative input sides
Use a screw driver to switch between Comparison and Subtraction mode
Acts the same as a vanilla comparator.
item.projectred_integration.and_cell_gat:
Has 1 input, 1 output and a pair of shared IO sides
Powring on the input and the pair will turn on the output.
item.projectred_integration.bus_randomiz:
Has 2 shared inputs and 1 bundled pseudo-input and 1 bundled output
Use a project red screwdriver to change between single and multi mode
In single mode, it transmits a pulse on only one of the channels in the bundled output. In multi mode, it transmits a pulse to any combination of channels in the bundled output. Requires one of the inputs to be on. Stoping the input will save the current state. The pseudo-input can be used to select which channels will be used by the randomizer.
item.projectred_integration.bus_converte:
Has 1 shared IO side, 2 output sides and 1 shared IO bundled side
Use a project red screw driver to switch between analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog mode
In analog-to-digital mode, it will output to the white channel if the shared IO side is not receiving any signal, otherwise it will output to the channel based on the redstone strength of the input.
In digital-to-analog mode, if at least one channel is active, then it will output on all analog outputs.
item.projectred_integration.bus_input_pa:
Has 1 bundled output and 1 input
Click on the buttons to output a redstone signal in the respective colored channel. Send a pulse to input to reset the panel.
item.projectred_integration.segment_disp:
Has 1 bundled input
Displays segements based on the active channels in the bundled inputs.
item.projectred_integration.dec_randomiz:
Has 1 input and 3 outputs
Use a scewdriver to disable the right output
When receiving a signal, randomly choses on of the outputs to enable.
item.projectred_integration.stacking_lat:
Has 1 input, 1 output and 1 pair of shared IO
THe pair is the write signal, and left is the keep signal. When the write signal receives a pulse, the state of the input will be stored and outputted. When the keep signal receives a pulse, the current state will be kept until the next write pulse. If the input receives a pulse while the write signal is active, it will change the stored state immediately without waiting for the next write pulse.
